content.text:
Let me play this out carefully — I want to actually stress-test the proposed end-state, not just describe it favorably.

I'll use a real recurring topic shape: **"the cost-optimization arc"** (c-mok4nke6 et al), which spans Apr 25 → Apr 29, four sessions, multiple sub-decisions, ongoing. That's the right grain for the test.

---

## Best case (with all proposed changes in place)

**Day 1, session 1.** Sean: *"Implementer cost is too high. Let's figure out what to do."* I create a top-level click `cost-opt`, immediately attach a body file `clicks/cost-opt.md` with a one-line goal at the top and an empty *Options* section. We talk; I append findings to the body as we go (29 candidate interventions, source data from rig analysis, compounding model). End of session: I parked it after writing a concise *State* section: "29 interventions surfaced, 3 priority bundles emerging (P1/P2/P3), no decisions yet." Index regenerates. Sean can open `clicks/index.md` and see `[parked] cost-opt — 29 interventions, 3 priority bundles emerging`.

**Day 2, session 2.** New Coco. Tree-touched-since-3d shows cost-opt at the top. Sean: *"Let's lock in P1 and dispatch it."* I run `extract --id cost-opt --include-body` — get the goal, status, body file inline, all in one read (~5K chars, but it's the *whole working state*, not noise). I create child `cost-opt.p1` with its own body file. We refine. I conclude p1 with a 200-char verdict ("inventory-excerpting via subagent; commissioned as w-xxx") because the rationale lives in the body. Body file appends: "**Resolution**: see verdict; full design in handoff doc X." Commission link auto-created. Index regenerates.

**Day 3, session 3.** Different topic entirely (Laboratory work). Cost-opt doesn't touch context. Tree doesn't show it because nothing happened in 3 days for that subtree. Sean's mental model is intact because he opened the index this morning and saw `[live] cost-opt — P1 dispatched, P2/P3 pending`.

**Day 5, session 4.** Sean: *"Status of P2?"* I run `extract --id cost-opt.p2`, see the body, see what's blocking. Sean reviews the body file directly in his editor while we talk, points at section 3, says "redo this analysis." I update the body, no click write needed. We conclude p2.

**What worked:**
- Body file is the single source of working state. No `.scratch/` orphans.
- Sean can browse and verify the index whenever he wants — and *did*, on day 3. Trust closes.
- Tree views are scoped to "what's actually moving." The 226-leaf live pile is invisible.
- Conclusions stay tight because the body absorbs the rationale.
- Multi-session handoff is cheap (~5K context for the whole topic state, vs. ~31K for sprawling extract).

**Worth ~**a 60% reduction in click-context cost and a much higher trust floor.

---

## Worst case (same scenario, what falls apart)

**Day 1.** Same start, but I forget to attach a body file at create time. We talk. I open 4 child clicks during the conversation, each with a 600-char goal trying to capture nuance. End of session: I conclude two of the children with 1,400-char essay conclusions because the body habit didn't take and there's no policy enforcing it. The other two children stay live with bloated goals. Index regenerates and shows 5 cost-opt clicks, all with verbose text — Sean's index becomes its own form of overwhelm.

**Day 2.** Different Coco session boots. Tree-touched-since-3d shows cost-opt. I run `extract --id cost-opt` — get 8K chars of bloated goals + essay conclusions, no body file because none exists. I'm back to "infer the state from prose fragments." Sean has read `clicks/index.md` between sessions and is unsure why some children have body links and some don't. He asks me to clean it up. I spend ~10 turns reorganizing — converting essay conclusions into body files post-hoc, which doesn't really work because conclusions are immutable. I end up creating supersedes-linked replacement clicks, doubling the count.

**Day 3.** Sean revisits. The supersedes links don't surface in tree view (still the known gap). Sean reads the index, sees both old and new clicks, asks "which is current?" I have to explain the supersedes mechanism. Trust erodes.

**Day 5.** A genuinely new sub-decision comes up (P4 — caching). I open `cost-opt.p4`. *Should it be a child of cost-opt or a child of P3?* No playbook answer. I improvise; Sean would have parented it differently. Mismatch. Sean asks why; I shrug; trust erodes again.

**What broke:**
- The body-file mechanism is *opt-in*. Without enforcement, I drift back to bloated text inside click fields. **The biggest fix in the proposal is the easiest to skip.**
- Supersedes findability stays broken; the long tail of fixes hits at the worst moments.
- The playbook gap shows up most painfully on judgment calls (granularity, parentage). A document doesn't fix improvisation drift; the playbook gets read once and forgotten.
- The index is only as good as the click hygiene behind it. Garbage-in, garbage-displayed.

The worst case reveals: **the proposed evolution is mostly additive, and additive fixes can be ignored.** Real change requires constraints, not options.

---

## What the worst case tells me

Three things stay broken even after all the proposed changes:

1. **Body file is opt-in.** I'll skip it under time pressure, then there's no clean recovery (immutable conclusions). The proposal has the right *shape* but the wrong *enforcement*.
2. **Three artifact types are jammed into one.** Real workload contains short atomic decisions, multi-day deliberations, and lightweight todos. One mechanism can't be optimal for all three. Body files are a partial answer to deliberations but make atomic decisions feel ceremonial.
3. **The playbook is a doc, not a guardrail.** Documents drift; constraints don't. I need *behavior shaped by the tool*, not by a doc I read once.

---

## Creative alternatives — radical shapes

**Alternative A: Threads + Records + Tasks (three artifact types).**

Stop pretending one mechanism fits everything. Three crisp shapes:

- **Threads** — long-form, mutable, conversational. File-backed at `threads/<slug>.md`. Where deliberation actually happens. Open / parked / archived lifecycle. No tree of threads — they're flat, with cross-links. Sean opens them in his editor.
- **Records** — short, immutable, frozen at creation. `records/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>.md`. Captures a decision *after* it's been deliberated. Format: verdict + 1-paragraph why + supersedes-link + commissioned-writ-link. Records are the "permanent ledger" — what we actually decided.
- **Tasks** — short, lightweight todos. DB-backed. Open / done / dropped. *No body, no rationale, no tree.* For "remember to revisit this" and nothing more.

Threads spawn records when something concludes. Tasks are independent. This dissolves the click-as-everything-bag problem.

**Best case for cost-opt under this model**: Day 1 opens `threads/cost-opt.md` (long-form, mutable, that's where we work). Day 2 lands record `records/2026-04-26-cost-opt-p1.md` (frozen, citable). Day 5 opens task "investigate caching as P4" if it's not yet thread-worthy. Each artifact type has crisp behavior.

**Worst case**: I misclassify (treat a thread as a record, get stuck because records are frozen). Mitigation: thread-to-record promotion is explicit and one-directional.

This is my favorite creative alternative.

**Alternative B: Sessions are first-class, topics emerge as tags.**

Every session has a charter (one line at start: "today we work on X") and an outcome (one paragraph at end: "we decided Y, deferred Z"). These are auto-captured. Topics are tags spanning sessions. The "tree" is just tag co-occurrence and supersedes-links. There's no atomic click decision unit — decisions live in session outcomes, made citable by id.

- **Pro**: No bookkeeping ceremony. The thing you do (have a session) is the thing that gets recorded.
- **Con**: Loses tree decomposition. Sub-questions become hard to surface as their own things.
- **Verdict**: Too much loss. Hierarchical decomposition is genuinely useful for our work.

**Alternative C: The "campaign" model.**

A campaign is a multi-session arc with charter + log + decisions + open-questions. Always file-backed. You don't have small things — small things stay in chat. Campaigns are deliberately heavyweight to open.

- **Pro**: Multi-session continuity becomes the *primary* design goal, not a side effect. Beautifully fits the cost-opt arc.
- **Con**: Mismatch for small inquiries — "should we add a flag here?" doesn't deserve a campaign. We'd end up needing a smaller artifact too. Which gets us back to multi-type.

**Alternative D: A daemon that watches conversation and proposes structure.**

Coco doesn't run `nsg click create` mid-conversation. A background process reads the transcript stream (Stacks CDC already does this for sessions) and proposes structure: *"This exchange looks like a decision. Promote to record? [y/n]"*. Promotion is a single keystroke. Coco doesn't write clicks during the flow; structure is harvested from the natural conversation afterward.

- **Pro**: Eliminates the micro-write cost almost entirely. Structure follows conversation, not the other way around.
- **Pro**: Sean stays in flow; Coco stays in flow; the system catches up at session-end.
- **Con**: Latency between decision and capture. False positives (proposes structure for chitchat). Requires a smart-enough observer.
- **This pairs well with Alternative A**: the daemon harvests threads → records.

---

## What I'd actually build if starting fresh

**Threads + Records + Tasks**, file-backed, with conversation-end harvesting via daemon.

Concrete shape:

- `threads/<slug>.md` — long-form, mutable, the working surface. Sean's editor IS the UI.
- `records/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>.md` — frozen decisions, citable, indexed.
- DB-backed tasks for lightweight todos (a stripped-down click — no body, no tree, no conclusion essay possible).
- A `nsg lab transcript-observer` that watches sessions and proposes structure at end-of-session ("looks like 3 decisions landed; want to file as records?"). One key per accept/reject.
- The "tree" Sean currently navigates becomes (a) the threads directory (alphabetical or recently-touched), (b) the records ledger (chronological), (c) the task list (kanban).
- Cross-cutting links via wikilinks (`[[record-id]]`) — git-grep is the query engine.

**The one thing this loses vs. clicks**: hard hierarchical decomposition. I think that's actually fine — the decomposition lives in thread structure (headers/sections in the body file), not in a separate id-tree. When a thread sub-question gets big enough to deserve its own artifact, it spawns a sibling thread with a wikilink, not a child.

**Migration cost from current clicks**: real but bounded. Existing concluded clicks → records (one-time export). Existing live clicks with no body → tasks (lightweight todos). Existing live clicks with substantive content → threads (export to markdown).

---

## Honest assessment

I led with "evolve, don't replace" earlier. After playing this out, I'm less sure. The proposed evolution (body files + index + playbook) is a 60% improvement in best case but degrades to 20% improvement in worst case, because the fixes are additive and skippable. The three-artifact model is more disruptive but the *shape* of each artifact constrains me toward right behavior — I can't write an essay-conclusion in a record because records have a defined format, and I can't ceremonially over-capture a todo because tasks have no body slot.

The thing I keep coming back to: **the current click is one mechanism trying to be three different things, and the friction lives in the mismatch.** Separating them cleanly is the move with the highest ceiling.

That said: this is a much bigger swing. Want to chew on it before we commit? I can also draft the three-artifact spec in more detail, or we can keep it as a parked alternative under the assessment click and try the evolutionary path first.

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Export clicks corpus into threads/ + records/ + todo.md as a comparison artifact."""

import json, os, re
from collections import defaultdict
from datetime import datetime
from pathlib import Path

ROOT = Path("/workspace/nexus-mk2")
THREADS_DIR = ROOT / "threads"
RECORDS_DIR = ROOT / "records"
TODO_FILE = ROOT / "todo.md"

THREADS_DIR.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
RECORDS_DIR.mkdir(exist_ok=True)

# Load all clicks
clicks = {}
for status in ["live", "parked", "concluded", "dropped"]:
    with open(f"/tmp/clicks_{status}.json") as f:
        for c in json.load(f):
            clicks[c["id"]] = c

# Build children map
children_of = defaultdict(list)
for c in clicks.values():
    pid = c.get("parentId")
    if pid:
        children_of[pid].append(c["id"])

# Sort children by createdAt for stability
for pid in children_of:
    children_of[pid].sort(key=lambda cid: clicks[cid].get("createdAt", ""))


def slugify(text, max_len=50):
    """Make a filename-safe slug from text."""
    text = text.lower()
    text = re.sub(r'[^\w\s-]', '', text)
    text = re.sub(r'[\s-]+', '-', text)
    text = text.strip('-')
    if len(text) > max_len:
        text = text[:max_len].rstrip('-')
    return text


def short_id(cid):
    """Short prefix of a click id for display."""
    # c-mo1itggx-433e7cd6bda1 → c-mo1itggx
    parts = cid.split("-")
    return "-".join(parts[:2]) if len(parts) >= 2 else cid


def first_sentences(text, n=3):
    """Take first N sentences of text."""
    if not text:
        return ""
    # Split on sentence-ending punctuation followed by space+capital or end
    sentences = re.split(r'(?<=[.!?])\s+(?=[A-Z(])', text.strip())
    return " ".join(sentences[:n])


def is_task_shaped(goal):
    """Heuristic: does this goal look like a discrete task vs. an open question/thread?"""
    if not goal:
        return False
    g = goal.strip()
    # Question word at start → not a task
    if re.match(r'^(How|What|When|Why|Where|Should|Could|Would|Can|Does|Do|Is|Are|Which|Who)\b', g, re.IGNORECASE):
        return False
    if g.startswith("?") or "?" in g.split("—")[0][:80]:
        # Has a question mark in the framing portion
        return False
    # Imperative verbs at start → task
    task_verbs = (
        r'^(Implement|Fix|Build|Add|Create|Update|Move|Replace|Remove|Delete|'
        r'Refactor|Ship|Land|Port|Dispatch|Register|Surface|Augment|Stand up|'
        r'Run|Make|Write|Document|Scope|Separate|Deliver|Capture|Convert|'
        r'Migrate|Extract|Consolidate|Rename|Drop|Reframe|Audit|Sweep|Wire|'
        r'Integrate|Enable|Resolve|Investigate|Diagnose|Detect|Recover|Auto-recover|'
        r'Rewrite|Backfill|Upgrade|Promote|Demote|Bundle|Unblock|Unwire|Inject|'
        r'Replace|Eliminate|Reduce|Optimize|Tighten|Loosen|Validate|Verify)\b'
    )
    if re.match(task_verbs, g, re.IGNORECASE):
        return True
    return False


def is_thread_active(cid, visited=None):
    """A thread (subtree rooted here) is active if root or any descendant is live/parked."""
    if visited is None:
        visited = set()
    if cid in visited:
        return False
    visited.add(cid)
    c = clicks.get(cid)
    if not c:
        return False
    if c["status"] in ("live", "parked"):
        return True
    return any(is_thread_active(child_id, visited) for child_id in children_of.get(cid, []))


def has_live_or_parked_descendant(cid, visited=None):
    if visited is None:
        visited = set()
    if cid in visited:
        return False
    visited.add(cid)
    for ch in children_of.get(cid, []):
        c = clicks.get(ch)
        if c and c["status"] in ("live", "parked"):
            return True
        if has_live_or_parked_descendant(ch, visited):
            return True
    return False


def render_thread_node(cid, depth, lines, todo_collector, parent_topic):
    """Render a click and its subtree into the thread file."""
    c = clicks.get(cid)
    if not c:
        return
    indent = "  " * (depth - 1) if depth > 0 else ""
    bullet = "-" if depth > 0 else "##"
    sid = short_id(cid)
    goal = c["goal"]
    status = c["status"]
    children = children_of.get(cid, [])

    # Classify this node:
    # 1. Concluded/dropped → decision summary + link to record
    # 2. Live + leaf + task-shaped → captured to todo, mention briefly here
    # 3. Live + leaf + question-shaped → open question
    # 4. Live + has children → sub-thread or section
    # 5. Parked → noted dormant

    if status in ("concluded", "dropped"):
        rec_path = record_path_for(cid)
        summary = first_sentences(c.get("conclusion", ""), 2)
        marker = "○" if status == "concluded" else "✕"
        if depth == 0:
            lines.append(f"## {marker} {goal}")
            lines.append("")
            lines.append(f"**Resolved**: {summary}")
            lines.append("")
            lines.append(f"→ Full record: [`{rec_path.name}`](../records/{rec_path.name})")
            lines.append("")
        else:
            lines.append(f"{indent}- {marker} **{goal}** — {summary} → [`{rec_path.name}`](../records/{rec_path.name})")
    elif status == "parked":
        if depth == 0:
            lines.append(f"## ◇ {goal}")
            lines.append(f"*Parked.*")
            lines.append("")
        else:
            lines.append(f"{indent}- ◇ *(parked)* {goal}")
    elif status == "live":
        is_leaf = len(children) == 0
        if is_leaf:
            if is_task_shaped(goal):
                # Goes to todo, mention briefly
                todo_collector.append((parent_topic, sid, goal))
                if depth > 0:
                    lines.append(f"{indent}- ☐ *(task → todo.md)* {goal}")
                else:
                    lines.append(f"## ● {goal}")
                    lines.append(f"*Task — see todo.md.*")
                    lines.append("")
            else:
                # Open question
                if depth == 0:
                    lines.append(f"## ● {goal}")
                    lines.append(f"*Open.*")
                    lines.append("")
                else:
                    lines.append(f"{indent}- ● {goal}")
        else:
            # Live with children → section header
            if depth == 0:
                lines.append(f"## ● {goal}")
                lines.append("")
            else:
                lines.append(f"{indent}- ● **{goal}**")

    # Recurse into children (for non-records-style display)
    if children and status != "concluded" and status != "dropped":
        for child_id in children:
            render_thread_node(child_id, depth + 1, lines, todo_collector, parent_topic)
    elif status in ("concluded", "dropped") and children:
        # Even concluded clicks may have children worth noting
        # Show them inline
        active_children = [ch for ch in children if is_thread_active(ch)]
        if active_children:
            lines.append("")
            lines.append("**Follow-on threads:**")
            lines.append("")
            for child_id in active_children:
                render_thread_node(child_id, 1, lines, todo_collector, parent_topic)


def record_path_for(cid):
    c = clicks.get(cid)
    if not c:
        return RECORDS_DIR / f"{short_id(cid)}.md"
    resolved = c.get("resolvedAt", "")
    date = resolved.split("T")[0] if resolved else "unknown"
    slug = slugify(c["goal"], max_len=50)
    return RECORDS_DIR / f"{date}-{slug}-{short_id(cid)}.md"


def write_record(cid):
    c = clicks.get(cid)
    if not c or c["status"] not in ("concluded", "dropped"):
        return
    path = record_path_for(cid)
    lines = []
    lines.append(f"# {c['goal']}")
    lines.append("")
    lines.append(f"- **id**: `{cid}`")
    lines.append(f"- **status**: {c['status']}")
    lines.append(f"- **resolved**: {c.get('resolvedAt', 'unknown')}")
    if c.get("parentId"):
        parent = clicks.get(c["parentId"])
        if parent:
            lines.append(f"- **context**: under [{parent['goal'][:80]}](../threads/{thread_filename_for_root(thread_root_of(c['parentId']))})")
    lines.append("")
    lines.append("## Decision")
    lines.append("")
    lines.append(c.get("conclusion", "(no conclusion text)"))
    lines.append("")
    path.write_text("\n".join(lines))


def thread_root_of(cid):
    """Walk up to top-level click (no parent or parent missing)."""
    seen = set()
    cur = cid
    while cur and cur not in seen:
        seen.add(cur)
        c = clicks.get(cur)
        if not c:
            return cid  # broken link, stop
        if not c.get("parentId"):
            return cur
        if c["parentId"] not in clicks:
            return cur  # orphan, treat as root
        cur = c["parentId"]
    return cur


def thread_filename_for_root(cid):
    c = clicks.get(cid)
    if not c:
        return f"unknown-{short_id(cid)}.md"
    slug = slugify(c["goal"], max_len=50)
    return f"{slug}-{short_id(cid)}.md"


# ============================================================
# Main export
# ============================================================

# Find all thread roots = top-level clicks that are active OR have an active subtree
roots = []
for cid, c in clicks.items():
    if c.get("parentId") and c["parentId"] in clicks:
        continue  # not a root
    roots.append(cid)

# Active roots = those with at least one live/parked node in subtree (or are themselves)
active_roots = [r for r in roots if is_thread_active(r)]
inactive_roots = [r for r in roots if not is_thread_active(r)]

# Sort active roots by most-recent activity (for now: by createdAt of root, descending)
active_roots.sort(key=lambda r: clicks[r].get("createdAt", ""), reverse=True)

todo_items = []  # list of (topic, sid, goal)

# Generate thread files (one per active root)
for root_id in active_roots:
    fname = thread_filename_for_root(root_id)
    path = THREADS_DIR / fname
    lines = []
    root = clicks[root_id]
    lines.append(f"# {root['goal']}")
    lines.append("")
    lines.append(f"- **root id**: `{root_id}`")
    lines.append(f"- **status**: {root['status']}")
    lines.append(f"- **created**: {root.get('createdAt', '')[:10]}")
    lines.append("")
    lines.append("---")
    lines.append("")
    # Render the subtree
    topic = root["goal"][:60]
    children = children_of.get(root_id, [])
    if not children:
        # Standalone root
        if root["status"] == "live" and is_task_shaped(root["goal"]):
            todo_items.append((topic, short_id(root_id), root["goal"]))
            lines.append(f"*Standalone task — see todo.md.*")
        else:
            lines.append(f"*Standalone {root['status']} click.*")
    else:
        for child_id in children:
            render_thread_node(child_id, 1, lines, todo_items, topic)
    lines.append("")
    path.write_text("\n".join(lines))

# Generate inactive-root thread files (smaller, archive-style)
inactive_dir = THREADS_DIR / "_archive"
inactive_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
for root_id in inactive_roots:
    if not children_of.get(root_id):
        # Skip standalone concluded clicks (they have records)
        continue
    fname = thread_filename_for_root(root_id)
    path = inactive_dir / fname
    lines = []
    root = clicks[root_id]
    lines.append(f"# {root['goal']}")
    lines.append("")
    lines.append(f"- **root id**: `{root_id}`")
    lines.append(f"- **status**: {root['status']} (archived — no live descendants)")
    lines.append("")
    lines.append("---")
    lines.append("")
    topic = root["goal"][:60]
    for child_id in children_of.get(root_id, []):
        render_thread_node(child_id, 1, lines, [], topic)  # discard todo from archive
    lines.append("")
    path.write_text("\n".join(lines))

# Generate records (concluded + dropped)
for cid, c in clicks.items():
    if c["status"] in ("concluded", "dropped"):
        write_record(cid)

# Generate todo.md
todo_lines = []
todo_lines.append("# Todo")
todo_lines.append("")
todo_lines.append(f"Live tasks extracted from the click corpus, grouped by source thread.")
todo_lines.append(f"Generated {datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M')}.")
todo_lines.append("")
# Group by topic
by_topic = defaultdict(list)
for topic, sid, goal in todo_items:
    by_topic[topic].append((sid, goal))
for topic in sorted(by_topic.keys()):
    todo_lines.append(f"## {topic}")
    todo_lines.append("")
    for sid, goal in by_topic[topic]:
        todo_lines.append(f"- [ ] **{goal}** (`{sid}`)")
    todo_lines.append("")
TODO_FILE.write_text("\n".join(todo_lines))

# Summary stats
print(f"Wrote:")
print(f"  threads/: {len(active_roots)} active thread files + {sum(1 for r in inactive_roots if children_of.get(r))} archived")
print(f"  records/: {sum(1 for c in clicks.values() if c['status'] in ('concluded','dropped'))} records")
print(f"  todo.md: {len(todo_items)} tasks")
print(f"")
print(f"Active roots (sample first 10):")
for r in active_roots[:10]:
    print(f"  {short_id(r)}: {clicks[r]['goal'][:70]}")

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Export clicks corpus into threads/ + records/ + todo.md as a comparison artifact."""

import json, os, re
from collections import defaultdict
from datetime import datetime
from pathlib import Path

ROOT = Path("/workspace/nexus-mk2")
THREADS_DIR = ROOT / "threads"
RECORDS_DIR = ROOT / "records"
TODO_FILE = ROOT / "todo.md"

THREADS_DIR.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
RECORDS_DIR.mkdir(exist_ok=True)

# Load all clicks
clicks = {}
for status in ["live", "parked", "concluded", "dropped"]:
    with open(f"/tmp/clicks_{status}.json") as f:
        for c in json.load(f):
            clicks[c["id"]] = c

# Build children map
children_of = defaultdict(list)
for c in clicks.values():
    pid = c.get("parentId")
    if pid:
        children_of[pid].append(c["id"])

# Sort children by createdAt for stability
for pid in children_of:
    children_of[pid].sort(key=lambda cid: clicks[cid].get("createdAt", ""))


def slugify(text, max_len=50):
    """Make a filename-safe slug from text."""
    text = text.lower()
    text = re.sub(r'[^\w\s-]', '', text)
    text = re.sub(r'[\s-]+', '-', text)
    text = text.strip('-')
    if len(text) > max_len:
        text = text[:max_len].rstrip('-')
    return text


def short_id(cid):
    """Short prefix of a click id for display."""
    # c-mo1itggx-433e7cd6bda1 → c-mo1itggx
    parts = cid.split("-")
    return "-".join(parts[:2]) if len(parts) >= 2 else cid


def first_sentences(text, n=3):
    """Take first N sentences of text."""
    if not text:
        return ""
    # Split on sentence-ending punctuation followed by space+capital or end
    sentences = re.split(r'(?<=[.!?])\s+(?=[A-Z(])', text.strip())
    return " ".join(sentences[:n])


def is_task_shaped(goal):
    """Heuristic: does this goal look like a discrete task vs. an open question/thread?"""
    if not goal:
        return False
    g = goal.strip()
    # Question word at start → not a task
    if re.match(r'^(How|What|When|Why|Where|Should|Could|Would|Can|Does|Do|Is|Are|Which|Who)\b', g, re.IGNORECASE):
        return False
    if g.startswith("?") or "?" in g.split("—")[0][:80]:
        # Has a question mark in the framing portion
        return False
    # Imperative verbs at start → task
    task_verbs = (
        r'^(Implement|Fix|Build|Add|Create|Update|Move|Replace|Remove|Delete|'
        r'Refactor|Ship|Land|Port|Dispatch|Register|Surface|Augment|Stand up|'
        r'Run|Make|Write|Document|Scope|Separate|Deliver|Capture|Convert|'
        r'Migrate|Extract|Consolidate|Rename|Drop|Reframe|Audit|Sweep|Wire|'
        r'Integrate|Enable|Resolve|Investigate|Diagnose|Detect|Recover|Auto-recover|'
        r'Rewrite|Backfill|Upgrade|Promote|Demote|Bundle|Unblock|Unwire|Inject|'
        r'Replace|Eliminate|Reduce|Optimize|Tighten|Loosen|Validate|Verify)\b'
    )
    if re.match(task_verbs, g, re.IGNORECASE):
        return True
    return False


def is_thread_active(cid, visited=None):
    """A thread (subtree rooted here) is active if root or any descendant is live/parked."""
    if visited is None:
        visited = set()
    if cid in visited:
        return False
    visited.add(cid)
    c = clicks.get(cid)
    if not c:
        return False
    if c["status"] in ("live", "parked"):
        return True
    return any(is_thread_active(child_id, visited) for child_id in children_of.get(cid, []))


def has_live_or_parked_descendant(cid, visited=None):
    if visited is None:
        visited = set()
    if cid in visited:
        return False
    visited.add(cid)
    for ch in children_of.get(cid, []):
        c = clicks.get(ch)
        if c and c["status"] in ("live", "parked"):
            return True
        if has_live_or_parked_descendant(ch, visited):
            return True
    return False


def render_thread_node(cid, depth, lines, todo_collector, parent_topic):
    """Render a click and its subtree into the thread file."""
    c = clicks.get(cid)
    if not c:
        return
    indent = "  " * (depth - 1) if depth > 0 else ""
    bullet = "-" if depth > 0 else "##"
    sid = short_id(cid)
    goal = c["goal"]
    status = c["status"]
    children = children_of.get(cid, [])

    # Classify this node:
    # 1. Concluded/dropped → decision summary + link to record
    # 2. Live + leaf + task-shaped → captured to todo, mention briefly here
    # 3. Live + leaf + question-shaped → open question
    # 4. Live + has children → sub-thread or section
    # 5. Parked → noted dormant

    if status in ("concluded", "dropped"):
        rec_path = record_path_for(cid)
        summary = first_sentences(c.get("conclusion", ""), 2)
        marker = "○" if status == "concluded" else "✕"
        if depth == 0:
            lines.append(f"## {marker} {goal}")
            lines.append("")
            lines.append(f"**Resolved**: {summary}")
            lines.append("")
            lines.append(f"→ Full record: [`{rec_path.name}`](../records/{rec_path.name})")
            lines.append("")
        else:
            lines.append(f"{indent}- {marker} **{goal}** — {summary} → [`{rec_path.name}`](../records/{rec_path.name})")
    elif status == "parked":
        if depth == 0:
            lines.append(f"## ◇ {goal}")
            lines.append(f"*Parked.*")
            lines.append("")
        else:
            lines.append(f"{indent}- ◇ *(parked)* {goal}")
    elif status == "live":
        is_leaf = len(children) == 0
        if is_leaf:
            if is_task_shaped(goal):
                # Goes to todo, mention briefly
                todo_collector.append((parent_topic, sid, goal))
                if depth > 0:
                    lines.append(f"{indent}- ☐ *(task → todo.md)* {goal}")
                else:
                    lines.append(f"## ● {goal}")
                    lines.append(f"*Task — see todo.md.*")
                    lines.append("")
            else:
                # Open question
                if depth == 0:
                    lines.append(f"## ● {goal}")
                    lines.append(f"*Open.*")
                    lines.append("")
                else:
                    lines.append(f"{indent}- ● {goal}")
        else:
            # Live with children → section header
            if depth == 0:
                lines.append(f"## ● {goal}")
                lines.append("")
            else:
                lines.append(f"{indent}- ● **{goal}**")

    # Recurse into children (for non-records-style display)
    if children and status != "concluded" and status != "dropped":
        for child_id in children:
            render_thread_node(child_id, depth + 1, lines, todo_collector, parent_topic)
    elif status in ("concluded", "dropped") and children:
        # Even concluded clicks may have children worth noting
        # Show them inline
        active_children = [ch for ch in children if is_thread_active(ch)]
        if active_children:
            lines.append("")
            lines.append("**Follow-on threads:**")
            lines.append("")
            for child_id in active_children:
                render_thread_node(child_id, 1, lines, todo_collector, parent_topic)


def record_path_for(cid):
    c = clicks.get(cid)
    if not c:
        return RECORDS_DIR / f"{short_id(cid)}.md"
    resolved = c.get("resolvedAt", "")
    date = resolved.split("T")[0] if resolved else "unknown"
    slug = slugify(c["goal"], max_len=50)
    return RECORDS_DIR / f"{date}-{slug}-{short_id(cid)}.md"


def write_record(cid):
    c = clicks.get(cid)
    if not c or c["status"] not in ("concluded", "dropped"):
        return
    path = record_path_for(cid)
    lines = []
    lines.append(f"# {c['goal']}")
    lines.append("")
    lines.append(f"- **id**: `{cid}`")
    lines.append(f"- **status**: {c['status']}")
    lines.append(f"- **resolved**: {c.get('resolvedAt', 'unknown')}")
    if c.get("parentId"):
        parent = clicks.get(c["parentId"])
        if parent:
            lines.append(f"- **context**: under [{parent['goal'][:80]}](../threads/{thread_filename_for_root(thread_root_of(c['parentId']))})")
    lines.append("")
    lines.append("## Decision")
    lines.append("")
    lines.append(c.get("conclusion", "(no conclusion text)"))
    lines.append("")
    path.write_text("\n".join(lines))


def thread_root_of(cid):
    """Walk up to top-level click (no parent or parent missing)."""
    seen = set()
    cur = cid
    while cur and cur not in seen:
        seen.add(cur)
        c = clicks.get(cur)
        if not c:
            return cid  # broken link, stop
        if not c.get("parentId"):
            return cur
        if c["parentId"] not in clicks:
            return cur  # orphan, treat as root
        cur = c["parentId"]
    return cur


def thread_filename_for_root(cid):
    c = clicks.get(cid)
    if not c:
        return f"unknown-{short_id(cid)}.md"
    slug = slugify(c["goal"], max_len=50)
    return f"{slug}-{short_id(cid)}.md"


# ============================================================
# Main export
# ============================================================

# Find all thread roots = top-level clicks that are active OR have an active subtree
roots = []
for cid, c in clicks.items():
    if c.get("parentId") and c["parentId"] in clicks:
        continue  # not a root
    roots.append(cid)

# Active roots = those with at least one live/parked node in subtree (or are themselves)
active_roots = [r for r in roots if is_thread_active(r)]
inactive_roots = [r for r in roots if not is_thread_active(r)]

# Sort active roots by most-recent activity (for now: by createdAt of root, descending)
active_roots.sort(key=lambda r: clicks[r].get("createdAt", ""), reverse=True)

todo_items = []  # list of (topic, sid, goal)

# Generate thread files (one per active root)
for root_id in active_roots:
    fname = thread_filename_for_root(root_id)
    path = THREADS_DIR / fname
    lines = []
    root = clicks[root_id]
    lines.append(f"# {root['goal']}")
    lines.append("")
    lines.append(f"- **root id**: `{root_id}`")
    lines.append(f"- **status**: {root['status']}")
    lines.append(f"- **created**: {root.get('createdAt', '')[:10]}")
    lines.append("")
    lines.append("---")
    lines.append("")
    # Render the subtree
    topic = root["goal"][:60]
    children = children_of.get(root_id, [])
    if not children:
        # Standalone root
        if root["status"] == "live" and is_task_shaped(root["goal"]):
            todo_items.append((topic, short_id(root_id), root["goal"]))
            lines.append(f"*Standalone task — see todo.md.*")

            lines.append(f"*Standalone {root['status']} click.*")
    else:
        for child_id in children:
            render_thread_node(child_id, 1, lines, todo_items, topic)
    lines.append("")
    path.write_text("\n".join(lines))

# Generate inactive-root thread files (smaller, archive-style)
inactive_dir = THREADS_DIR / "_archive"
inactive_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
for root_id in inactive_roots:
    if not children_of.get(root_id):
        # Skip standalone concluded clicks (they have records)
        continue
    fname = thread_filename_for_root(root_id)
    path = inactive_dir / fname
    lines = []
    root = clicks[root_id]
    lines.append(f"# {root['goal']}")
    lines.append("")
    lines.append(f"- **root id**: `{root_id}`")
    lines.append(f"- **status**: {root['status']} (archived — no live descendants)")
    lines.append("")
    lines.append("---")
    lines.append("")
    topic = root["goal"][:60]
    for child_id in children_of.get(root_id, []):
        render_thread_node(child_id, 1, lines, [], topic)  # discard todo from archive
    lines.append("")
    path.write_text("\n".join(lines))

for cid, c in clicks.items():
    if c["status"] in ("concluded", "dropped"):
        write_record(cid)

# Generate todo.md
todo_lines = []
todo_lines.append("# Todo")
todo_lines.append("")
todo_lines.append(f"Live tasks extracted from the click corpus, grouped by source thread.")
todo_lines.append(f"Generated {datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M')}.")
todo_lines.append("")
# Group by topic
by_topic = defaultdict(list)
for topic, sid, goal in todo_items:
    by_topic[topic].append((sid, goal))
for topic in sorted(by_topic.keys()):
    todo_lines.append(f"## {topic}")
    todo_lines.append("")
    for sid, goal in by_topic[topic]:
        todo_lines.append(f"- [ ] **{goal}** (`{sid}`)")
    todo_lines.append("")
TODO_FILE.write_text("\n".join(todo_lines))

# Summary stats
print(f"Wrote:")
print(f"  threads/: {len(active_roots)} active thread files + {sum(1 for r in inactive_roots if children_of.get(r))} archived")
print(f"  records/: {sum(1 for c in clicks.values() if c['status'] in ('concluded','dropped'))} records")
print(f"  todo.md: {len(todo_items)} tasks")

    print(f"  {short_id(r)}: {clicks[r]['goal'][:70]}")

## Audit the /testing subpath surface across plugin packages —

## Build the Laboratory apparatus — a general-purpose toolkit f

- [ ] **Port the first real-world trial — pick whichever P3 experiment is next (likely X016 orientation suppression), author its manifest, run it through the apparatus end-to-end. Validates the apparatus on real research work and produces the first real findings via the new pipeline.** (`c-momaab8y`)

## Diagnose MCP tool-server 500s dropping mid-session on astrol

- [ ] **Diagnose MCP tool-server 500s dropping mid-session on astrolabe reader** (`c-mo1mqlvl`)

## Drop orphan SQLite indexes on writs book left over from the 

- [ ] **Drop orphan SQLite indexes on writs book left over from the status→phase rename (idx_clerk_writs_status and its compound variants). Out-of-band cleanup — no new commission; a Stacks-layer declarative index-drop mechanism is the proper long-term fix when another commission warrants it.** (`c-mo39vak3`)

## How should Coco's click-based session continuity work end-to

- [ ] **Surface supersedes links prominently in click tree/extract views regardless of parentage — make post-conclusion amendments findable from the original's neighborhood even when the superseding click is parented elsewhere** (`c-mobzwczn`)

## Planning quality — improve Astrolabe along its dimensions: d

- [ ] **Update Sage (sage-reader-analyst) instructions to consume Open-Questions handler outputs as additional primary input. Whatever handler design wins (a/b/c/d), it produces provenance-tagged supplements (patron-fabricated answers, bold-agent decisions, expert-panel recommendations, etc.) that must integrate into the Sage's working contract alongside the distillate. Open questions: do these supplements live as separate document sections or merge into the distillate? How does Sage weight them vs. the distillate's directly-distilled content? How do they affect Sage's existing brief-consultation rules (per c-mo3qsanr)?** (`c-mo4anoev`)
- [ ] **Migrate existing terminology to petition→brief→spec naming (post c-mo3qsanr decision on 2026-04-18). Scope: rename .claude/skills/briefs/ → petitions skill with content updated to reflect petition as the patron-input artifact; update bin/commission.sh and any other scripts that use 'brief' in the input sense; update Coco's CLAUDE.md briefs-skill references; sweep docs/ and experiments/ for input-sense 'brief' usage that should change. Out of scope: rewriting historical click conclusions (write-once) — the vocab tome carries the mapping. Also out of scope: renaming the Sage role in code/configs (no Sage code exists yet).** (`c-mo4bkd63`)

## Rethink scope — from upfront patron gate to downstream struc

- [ ] **Drop the upfront scope-items-as-patron-gate — the current scope section that gets emitted before decisions for patron inclusion/exclusion review** (`c-mo3d729h`)

## Scope daemon end-to-end integration test coverage

- [ ] **Scope daemon end-to-end integration test coverage** (`c-mo1mqlju`)

## Separate writs (obligations) from rigs (attempts) — the mult

- [ ] **Resolve the seal-engine workspace conflict — session container vs bare clone on host** (`c-mo3ibfq3`)
- [ ] **Move rig construction from Spider to the Fabricator** (`c-mo3ibgzt`)

## Unlock autonomous hopper-based operation for Nexus

- [ ] **Resolve plan-vs-plan conflicts when two Astrolabe sessions overlap** (`c-mo1mqcwz`)

content.content:
1	# How should Coco's click-based session continuity work end-to-end?
2	
3	- **root id**: `c-mo1itggx-433e7cd6bda1`
4	- **status**: parked
5	- **created**: 2026-04-16
6	
7	---
8	
9	- ● How do we prevent concluded clicks from becoming forgotten knowledge — decisions made but invisible to future sessions?
10	- ● Should the Oculus click page include graph visualization of the click tree?
11	- ○ **Should click goals be editable? (Immutability was an explicit design decision, but drift is a real cost)** — Yes, but only while live. Goals should be editable via 'nsg click amend --goal' while status=live, with goal_history preserved on the click for audit. → [`2026-04-23-should-click-goals-be-editable-immutability-was-an-c-mo1yf2y1.md`](../records/2026-04-23-should-click-goals-be-editable-immutability-was-an-c-mo1yf2y1.md)
12	- ○ **Augment `click show` with full ancestry + per-ancestor size info, or is `tree` + `extract` sufficient?** — Resolved via a different fix path. The real root cause of show-walking behavior was that `click extract` dropped conclusions by default — Coco would correctly reach for extract but still have to fall back to `show` walks to recover decision content. → [`2026-04-17-augment-click-show-with-full-ancestry-per-ancestor-c-mo2xtsqp.md`](../records/2026-04-17-augment-click-show-with-full-ancestry-per-ancestor-c-mo2xtsqp.md)
13	- ○ **Design the canonical vocabulary-alias registry — machine-readable map for resolving renames and drift across clicks, briefs, docs, and commits, companion to the narrative vocabulary tome** — Shipped. docs/future/vocabulary-aliases.yaml stood up as the machine-readable companion to guild-vocabulary.md, with schema supporting three relationship kinds (renames, subsumes, drops) plus optional changed_at, note, tome anchor, and see-also click refs. Seeded with 7 entries covering known drift (Reckoner/Overseer, Petition/Brief, Distiller/BA-PO, Sage/analyst, Pulse/missive, Lattice/Herald, manifest/summon-compose). → [`2026-04-23-design-the-canonical-vocabulary-alias-registry-mac-c-mobzw7uc.md`](../records/2026-04-23-design-the-canonical-vocabulary-alias-registry-mac-c-mobzw7uc.md)
14	- ○ **Enable goal editing on live clicks only (nsg click amend), preserving goal_history; seal the goal on conclude/drop** — Dispatched as commission w-moc1qlq7-e87e03fa2906 in codex nexus. Final shape: amend is live-only (parked/concluded/dropped rejected), each amend appends the prior goal to goal_history on the click, goal seals on terminal transition, 'nsg click amend' is the CLI surface, 'nsg click show' exposes history. → [`2026-04-23-enable-goal-editing-on-live-clicks-only-nsg-click-c-mobzw8pn.md`](../records/2026-04-23-enable-goal-editing-on-live-clicks-only-nsg-click-c-mobzw8pn.md)
15	- ○ **Document 'supersedes' as the canonical post-conclusion correction pattern — update the clicks skill and guidance with when/how to open a new click linked 'supersedes' to a concluded one** — Documented in .claude/skills/clicks/SKILL.md. Added 'Supersedes is the canonical post-conclusion correction pattern' subsection under Links — covers the three-step flow (create new click, link supersedes to old, conclude new click), when to reach for it (concluded decision wrong/incomplete/superseded by new info), when not to (rephrasing live → amend; vocab drift → alias registry; lateral cross-refs → related; dependency → depends-on), parentage guidance (parent where the new inquiry belongs; the link carries the trail), and an honest callout on the current findability gap (tree/extract don't surface inbound supersedes; name the old click id in the new conclusion as a prose backlink until c-mobzwczn lands). → [`2026-04-23-document-supersedes-as-the-canonical-post-conclusi-c-mobzw9of.md`](../records/2026-04-23-document-supersedes-as-the-canonical-post-conclusi-c-mobzw9of.md)
16	
17	**Follow-on threads:**
18	
19	- ☐ *(task → todo.md)* Surface supersedes links prominently in click tree/extract views regardless of parentage — make post-conclusion amendments findable from the original's neighborhood even when the superseding click is parented elsewhere
20	- ◇ *(parked)* Click-tool assessment 2026-04-29 — quantitative review (10 transcripts, ~14% context cost, 1.4% tree-utility, 1064-char mean conclusion, 291 live clicks) + Sean's pain (no long-form home, lost-in-scratch, no mental model / can't verify, no standardized playbook, LLM impedance from micro-edit cadence). Verdict: substrate is right, click shape is wrong — collapsed short atomic decisions and long-form deliberations into one type that suits neither. Evolution path: separate structural skeleton from content body (hybrid: click + optional attached body file). Full assessment + 9 ranked recommendations + 4 open questions: docs/future/clicks-assessment-2026-04-29.md.

# How should Coco's click-based session continuity work end-to-end?

- **root id**: `c-mo1itggx-433e7cd6bda1`
- **status**: parked
- **created**: 2026-04-16

---

- ● How do we prevent concluded clicks from becoming forgotten knowledge — decisions made but invisible to future sessions?
- ● Should the Oculus click page include graph visualization of the click tree?
- ○ **Should click goals be editable? (Immutability was an explicit design decision, but drift is a real cost)** — Yes, but only while live. Goals should be editable via 'nsg click amend --goal' while status=live, with goal_history preserved on the click for audit. → [`2026-04-23-should-click-goals-be-editable-immutability-was-an-c-mo1yf2y1.md`](../records/2026-04-23-should-click-goals-be-editable-immutability-was-an-c-mo1yf2y1.md)
- ○ **Augment `click show` with full ancestry + per-ancestor size info, or is `tree` + `extract` sufficient?** — Resolved via a different fix path. The real root cause of show-walking behavior was that `click extract` dropped conclusions by default — Coco would correctly reach for extract but still have to fall back to `show` walks to recover decision content. → [`2026-04-17-augment-click-show-with-full-ancestry-per-ancestor-c-mo2xtsqp.md`](../records/2026-04-17-augment-click-show-with-full-ancestry-per-ancestor-c-mo2xtsqp.md)
- ○ **Design the canonical vocabulary-alias registry — machine-readable map for resolving renames and drift across clicks, briefs, docs, and commits, companion to the narrative vocabulary tome** — Shipped. docs/future/vocabulary-aliases.yaml stood up as the machine-readable companion to guild-vocabulary.md, with schema supporting three relationship kinds (renames, subsumes, drops) plus optional changed_at, note, tome anchor, and see-also click refs. Seeded with 7 entries covering known drift (Reckoner/Overseer, Petition/Brief, Distiller/BA-PO, Sage/analyst, Pulse/missive, Lattice/Herald, manifest/summon-compose). → [`2026-04-23-design-the-canonical-vocabulary-alias-registry-mac-c-mobzw7uc.md`](../records/2026-04-23-design-the-canonical-vocabulary-alias-registry-mac-c-mobzw7uc.md)
- ○ **Enable goal editing on live clicks only (nsg click amend), preserving goal_history; seal the goal on conclude/drop** — Dispatched as commission w-moc1qlq7-e87e03fa2906 in codex nexus. Final shape: amend is live-only (parked/concluded/dropped rejected), each amend appends the prior goal to goal_history on the click, goal seals on terminal transition, 'nsg click amend' is the CLI surface, 'nsg click show' exposes history. → [`2026-04-23-enable-goal-editing-on-live-clicks-only-nsg-click-c-mobzw8pn.md`](../records/2026-04-23-enable-goal-editing-on-live-clicks-only-nsg-click-c-mobzw8pn.md)
- ○ **Document 'supersedes' as the canonical post-conclusion correction pattern — update the clicks skill and guidance with when/how to open a new click linked 'supersedes' to a concluded one** — Documented in .claude/skills/clicks/SKILL.md. Added 'Supersedes is the canonical post-conclusion correction pattern' subsection under Links — covers the three-step flow (create new click, link supersedes to old, conclude new click), when to reach for it (concluded decision wrong/incomplete/superseded by new info), when not to (rephrasing live → amend; vocab drift → alias registry; lateral cross-refs → related; dependency → depends-on), parentage guidance (parent where the new inquiry belongs; the link carries the trail), and an honest callout on the current findability gap (tree/extract don't surface inbound supersedes; name the old click id in the new conclusion as a prose backlink until c-mobzwczn lands). → [`2026-04-23-document-supersedes-as-the-canonical-post-conclusi-c-mobzw9of.md`](../records/2026-04-23-document-supersedes-as-the-canonical-post-conclusi-c-mobzw9of.md)

**Follow-on threads:**

- ☐ *(task → todo.md)* Surface supersedes links prominently in click tree/extract views regardless of parentage — make post-conclusion amendments findable from the original's neighborhood even when the superseding click is parented elsewhere
- ◇ *(parked)* Click-tool assessment 2026-04-29 — quantitative review (10 transcripts, ~14% context cost, 1.4% tree-utility, 1064-char mean conclusion, 291 live clicks) + Sean's pain (no long-form home, lost-in-scratch, no mental model / can't verify, no standardized playbook, LLM impedance from micro-edit cadence). Verdict: substrate is right, click shape is wrong — collapsed short atomic decisions and long-form deliberations into one type that suits neither. Evolution path: separate structural skeleton from content body (hybrid: click + optional attached body file). Full assessment + 9 ranked recommendations + 4 open questions: docs/future/clicks-assessment-2026-04-29.md.

1	# Build the Laboratory apparatus — a general-purpose toolkit for running experiments on guild configurations. Audiences: nexus development (cost/quality tuning) and end users (evaluating their own prompts/plugins/configs). Architecture: experiment as first-class research unit; trial-runs as writ-shaped execution units; fixtures (setup/teardown with a dep DAG) decoupled from probes (data extraction); declarative scenarios + end-conditions; per-experiment analysis instruments. Lab runs in a 'real' guild that owns all experiment data; temp test-guilds are pure observation surfaces. Generalizes the P3 infrastructure spec (c-molveg89) into a reusable apparatus. Reuses the 'Laboratory' name (data-mirroring stub retired 2026-04-30 is internal-only).
2	
3	- **root id**: `c-mom18vmh-c1260c725e9f`
4	- **status**: live
5	- **created**: 2026-04-30
6	
7	---
8	
9	- ○ **Is 'experiment' a writ type, or do experiment-and-trial split into separate concepts?** — Split: experiment is the higher-level research unit (design + verdict); a trial-run is the writ-shaped execution unit (one apparatus instantiation, one scenario, one capture). One experiment fans out to N trial writs. → [`2026-04-30-is-experiment-a-writ-type-or-do-experiment-and-tri-c-mom18yxq.md`](../records/2026-04-30-is-experiment-a-writ-type-or-do-experiment-and-tri-c-mom18yxq.md)
10	- ○ **Where do experiment-generated data live — in temp/test guilds, sanctum, or the lab-host guild?** — All experiment-generated data lives in the 'real' guild that hosts the Laboratory apparatus. Temp test-guilds are pure observation surfaces — set up, poked at, dumped, destroyed — never authoritative storage. → [`2026-04-30-where-do-experiment-generated-data-live-in-temptes-c-mom18zkt.md`](../records/2026-04-30-where-do-experiment-generated-data-live-in-temptes-c-mom18zkt.md)
11	- ○ **Reuse the 'Laboratory' name for the new apparatus, or pick a fresh one to avoid confusion with the retired data-mirroring plugin?** — Keep 'Laboratory.' The retired plugin's meaning was internal to Sean+Coco — no users carry mental models of it. The name is the clearest signal of intent for future users of the apparatus. → [`2026-04-30-reuse-the-laboratory-name-for-the-new-apparatus-or-c-mom1907v.md`](../records/2026-04-30-reuse-the-laboratory-name-for-the-new-apparatus-or-c-mom1907v.md)
12	- ○ **Decompose 'fixture' into Fixture (lifecycle: setup/teardown) + Probe (data extraction), or keep the operations fused on a single concept?** — Split. Fixtures handle setup/teardown with an explicit dep DAG; probes extract data from one or more fixtures and run independently of the fixture lifecycle. → [`2026-04-30-decompose-fixture-into-fixture-lifecycle-setuptear-c-mom19117.md`](../records/2026-04-30-decompose-fixture-into-fixture-lifecycle-setuptear-c-mom19117.md)
13	- ○ **Fixture scope/lifetime — trial-scoped (fresh per trial) vs experiment-scoped (shared across trials) vs ad-hoc? How is scope declared and enforced, and does it interact with the dep DAG (e.g., a trial-scoped fixture depending on an experiment-scoped one)?** — MVP0 ships trial-scope only — every fixture is fresh per trial, torn down at trial end. Reserve hooks for v2 without implementing: 'scope' field on fixture declarations (only 'trial' valid in v1), 'mutability' field ('mutable | read-only | snapshotted', informational in v1), DAG validator stub aware of 'lab > experiment > trial' ordering. → [`2026-05-01-fixture-scopelifetime-trial-scoped-fresh-per-trial-c-mom19282.md`](../records/2026-05-01-fixture-scopelifetime-trial-scoped-fresh-per-trial-c-mom19282.md)
14	- ○ **How do we declaratively express the scenario — the workload that runs inside a trial after setup (commissions to post, ordering, end-conditions like queue-drains/timeout/rig-completes)? The spec doc currently assumes a human posts commissions manually; a real toolkit needs a scenario primitive.** — Scenario IS a rig template, dispatched via existing Spider/Reckoner machinery. No separate scenario primitive. → [`2026-05-01-how-do-we-declaratively-express-the-scenario-the-w-c-mom193ka.md`](../records/2026-05-01-how-do-we-declaratively-express-the-scenario-the-w-c-mom193ka.md)
15	- ○ **How is trial config carried into the rig — writ body, ext, or both?** — ext.laboratory.config carries the structured trial config (fixtures, plugin pins, files, brief refs, probe selections). Body stays free-form prose for human description (variant intent, hypothesis, notes). → [`2026-05-01-how-is-trial-config-carried-into-the-rig-writ-body-c-mom9vkls.md`](../records/2026-05-01-how-is-trial-config-carried-into-the-rig-writ-body-c-mom9vkls.md)
16	- ◇ *(parked)* Cross-guild engine surface — when does shell-out via 'nsg --guild-root <other>' stop being good enough? MVP path is CLI shell-out; track for symptoms (auth, perf, observability across the boundary) that motivate a real cross-guild engine surface.
17	- ● **Implementation — turn the Laboratory MVP0 design into a working apparatus. First few items (framework verification, plugin reshape, schema/CLI) unblock everything else; engines and archive can parallelize once the skeleton lands.**
18	  - ○ **Verify framework prerequisites — confirm 'nsg init', 'nsg plugin install' (does it accept git SHAs / refs, not just npm semver?), and 'nsg codex add' invocation surfaces against current framework source. Confirm gh org permissions for codex repo create/delete. Surface gaps to negotiate with framework before scripts land.** — All load-bearing prerequisites confirmed against live framework source: non-anima engines first-class via { status: 'completed' } EngineRunResult (precedent: spider.seal does git ops sync and returns yields); 'nsg plugin install' accepts SHAs natively via npm git+URL#sha syntax; ClerkApi.registerWritType is the writ-type contribution path (called from apparatus.start); SpiderKit.rigTemplates + rigTemplateMappings contribute templates+routings; Fabricator scans kit.engines for designs. One spec correction: 'nsg codex add' takes only (name, remoteUrl) — no --sha flag; pinning happens earlier by pushing fresh GH repo at the desired SHA before codex-add. → [`2026-05-01-verify-framework-prerequisites-confirm-nsg-init-ns-c-moma9psb.md`](../records/2026-05-01-verify-framework-prerequisites-confirm-nsg-init-ns-c-moma9psb.md)
19	  - ○ **Reshape the laboratory plugin from no-op stub to apparatus skeleton — package structure, kit-contribution exports, plugin metadata, TS types module. Foundation that subsequent children build on.** — Apparatus skeleton landed in /workspace/nexus-mk2/packages/laboratory: requires=[stacks,clerk], recommends=[spider,fabricator,codexes], supportKit empty (engines+templates+tools land in subsequent children), start() registers TRIAL_WRIT_TYPE_CONFIG via clerk.registerWritType. types.ts exports TRIAL_TYPE_NAME, TRIAL_WRIT_TYPE_CONFIG (mirrors mandate state machine: new → open → completed/failed/cancelled with stuck off open; childrenBehavior populated for v2 experiment-parent compatibility), and the LaboratoryTrialConfig schema (fixtures, scenario, probes, archive — provisional shapes; refined as engine work progresses). Legacy CDC data-mirroring code purged: dispatch-removal.test.ts and loom-composition.test.ts (orphan verification scripts for completed framework commissions) deleted; old LaboratoryConfig replaced with empty placeholder. → [`2026-05-01-reshape-the-laboratory-plugin-from-no-op-stub-to-a-c-moma9qyz.md`](../records/2026-05-01-reshape-the-laboratory-plugin-from-no-op-stub-to-a-c-moma9qyz.md)
20	  - ○ **Define the trial writ type and 'ext.laboratory.config' schema — TS types + JSON Schema, registered as a writ type in the lab's kit contribution. Schema mirrors the existing run.yaml shape from experiments/infrastructure/setup-and-artifacts.md.** — Trial writ type and ext.laboratory.config schema landed in packages/laboratory/src/types.ts. TRIAL_WRIT_TYPE_CONFIG mirrors mandate's state machine (new → open → completed/failed/cancelled with stuck off open) plus children-cascade triggers populated for v2 experiment-parent compatibility. → [`2026-05-01-define-the-trial-writ-type-and-extlaboratoryconfig-c-moma9sg5.md`](../records/2026-05-01-define-the-trial-writ-type-and-extlaboratoryconfig-c-moma9sg5.md)
21	  - ○ **Author manifest YAML format and the 'nsg lab trial post --manifest <file>' CLI — manifest schema (same shape as ext.laboratory.config plus optional 'description' field), parser, validator, writ-post via existing CLI surfaces.** — Manifest CLI landed: src/manifest.ts (zod schema mirroring LaboratoryTrialConfig with optional title, description, parentId, codex), parser (YAML → schema validation → fixture DAG cross-validation via topoSortFixtures → probe id uniqueness), and manifestToWritShape (splits into clerk.post inputs + ext.laboratory.config payload). src/tools/trial-post.ts wraps the parser and chains: clerk.post(type='trial') → clerk.setWritExt('laboratory', {config}) → clerk.transition('new'→'open') unless --draft. Codex auto-resolution mirrors commission-post (inherit from parentId; default to single registered codex; fail when multiple/none). 26 manifest tests added (55 total): YAML parse errors, schema rejections (slug shape, missing required, malformed ids, mutability enum), defaults (empty fixtures/probes, empty givens), cross-field DAG validation passthrough, probe id uniqueness, manifestToWritShape (title default 'Trial: <slug>', description→body, parentId/codex pass-through, trialConfig packing). → [`2026-05-01-author-manifest-yaml-format-and-the-nsg-lab-trial-c-moma9ty6.md`](../records/2026-05-01-author-manifest-yaml-format-and-the-nsg-lab-trial-c-moma9ty6.md)
22	  - ○ **Author the post-and-collect-default rig template — composes fixture-setup, scenario, probe, teardown, and archive engines from the writ's ext.laboratory.config. Topological sort of fixture deps happens at template instantiation; rig itself stays a flat sequence.** — post-and-collect-default rig template landed with the dynamic-graft pattern (same shape as spider.implement-loop): the static template declares one head engine ('lab.orchestrate') reading the writ; orchestrate at runtime reads ext.laboratory.config, topo-sorts fixtures, and emits the per-trial graft chain (fixture-setup → scenario → probes → archive → fixture-teardown-in-reverse) with sequential teardowns to keep teardown failures from shredding sibling teardowns. SupportKit wiring complete: rigTemplates['post-and-collect-default'] + rigTemplateMappings.trial. → [`2026-05-01-author-the-post-and-collect-default-rig-template-c-c-moma9vrm.md`](../records/2026-05-01-author-the-post-and-collect-default-rig-template-c-c-moma9vrm.md)

Local-bare-repo flow per Sean: setup clones upstream → checkout baseSha → init bare at <labHost>/.nexus/laboratory/codexes/<name>.git → push → register via Scriptorium.add. → [`2026-05-01-implement-codex-fixture-engines-setup-teardown-clo-c-moma9y1k.md`](../records/2026-05-01-implement-codex-fixture-engines-setup-teardown-clo-c-moma9y1k.md)

Refuses to overwrite an existing guild dir on setup; refuses to teardown if archive hasn't run.** — Real implementations of lab.guild-setup + lab.guild-teardown landed (engines/guild-fixture.ts). Setup: nsg init <path> → per-pin nsg plugin install → codex registration via duck-type discovery (any upstream yield with codexName+remoteUrl gets registered) → deep-merge guild.json → file copies (absolute sourcePath only in v1; manifest-relative resolution is future polish). → [`2026-05-01-implement-guild-fixture-engines-setup-teardown-nsg-c-momaa03d.md`](../records/2026-05-01-implement-guild-fixture-engines-setup-teardown-nsg-c-momaa03d.md)

Sequential commission posting only in v1; concurrent posting deferred.** — Cross-guild scenario engines landed (engines/scenario-xguild.ts). lab.commission-post-xguild posts a commission via 'nsg --guild-root <test-guild> commission-post', then INLINE polls until terminal (default waitForTerminal=true) — single engine fits the rig template's single-scenario-engine slot for the canonical 'post and wait' flow. Optional opt-out via waitForTerminal=false yields just {writId, postedAt}. → [`2026-05-01-implement-cross-guild-scenario-engines-commission-c-momaa1vt.md`](../records/2026-05-01-implement-cross-guild-scenario-engines-commission-c-momaa1vt.md)
26	  - ○ **Implement the three standard probe engines — lab.probe-stacks-dump (one row per test-guild source-row to lab-trial-stacks-dumps; pure JSON1 indexing), lab.probe-git-range (one row per commit to lab-trial-codex-commits; 10MB per-diff cap as fail-loud tripwire), lab.probe-trial-context (rig id + template name + framework SHA + resolved plugin pins + manifest snapshot as summary-only data; no bulk book). Each probe owns its book schema, run() handler, and extract() handler under src/probes/<name>/, and registers with the probe registry (c-momkil4p). Spec: packages/laboratory/README.md.** — Three standard probes implemented at packages/laboratory/src/probes/{trial-context,stacks-dump,git-range}.ts. lab.probe-trial-context: summary-only (no book), captures trialId/rigId/rigTemplate/frameworkVersion/pluginsInstalled/manifestSnapshot/capturedAt. lab.probe-stacks-dump: opens the test guild's nexus.db read-only via better-sqlite3 (already a transitive dep through Stacks), enumerates books_* tables from sqlite_master, writes one row per source-row to lab-trial-stacks-dumps with sourceBook resolution via prefix-match against the test guild's installed plugin list (preserves '<owner>/<book>' form when resolvable, raw books_… table name otherwise); summary {bookCounts: {...}, totalRows, capturedAt}. lab.probe-git-range: walks  in the codex bare repo, captures one row per commit (with --shortstat parsing for filesChanged/insertions/deletions), fails loud on any single diff > 10MB tripwire, summary {codexName, baseSha, headSha, commitCount, totalDiffBytes}. Each probe has a colocated extract() method that materializes its captured data: trial-context.yaml at top level; stacks-export/<safe-stem>.json per source book; codex-history/{commits-manifest.yaml, NNNN-<sha-12>.patch} for git range. → [`2026-05-01-implement-the-three-standard-probe-engines-labprob-c-momaa3w7.md`](../records/2026-05-01-implement-the-three-standard-probe-engines-labprob-c-momaa3w7.md)

Archive engine owns one minimal index book — lab-trial-archives — with row shape { id, trialId (FK to clerk/writs), status, archivedAt, probes[{id, engineId, summary}] }; archive engine has no schema opinions about probe data. → [`2026-05-01-design-and-implement-the-archive-engine-open-sub-q-c-momaa5o9.md`](../records/2026-05-01-design-and-implement-the-archive-engine-open-sub-q-c-momaa5o9.md)

Strategy chosen: synthetic-engine pipeline test, NOT full Spider/Animator stack. → [`2026-05-01-smoke-test-run-a-synthetic-trial-end-to-end-throug-c-momaa75l.md`](../records/2026-05-01-smoke-test-run-a-synthetic-trial-end-to-end-throug-c-momaa75l.md)
29	  - ○ **Retire and migrate experiments/infrastructure/setup-and-artifacts.md — the apparatus replaces the spec's standalone bash scripts. Decide: delete the spec doc, archive it as historical, or rewrite it as a usage guide pointing at the apparatus. Conclude c-molveg89 in the same step.** — Decision: archive-as-historical, not delete. Moved experiments/infrastructure/setup-and-artifacts.md to docs/archive/deprecated-docs/experimental-infrastructure-setup-and-artifacts.md with a top-of-file header pointing forward to the apparatus and packages/laboratory/README.md. → [`2026-05-01-retire-and-migrate-experimentsinfrastructuresetup-c-momaa8mk.md`](../records/2026-05-01-retire-and-migrate-experimentsinfrastructuresetup-c-momaa8mk.md)
30	  - ○ **Documentation — manifest authoring guide, laboratory plugin overview for /workspace/nexus/docs/architecture/, end-user how-to for evaluating prompts/plugins via trials. Audiences: nexus dev (architecture), end users (plugin docs).** — Documentation pass complete. Fleshed out packages/laboratory/README.md's Authoring trials section with: a complete minimal manifest example (all four engine pairs + three probes wired), manifest-shape rules (slug/id/DAG/teardown override/givens), the post-trial CLI workflow (writ list / show, rig list, lab trial-show / extract / export-book), and authoring tips (codex baseSha picks the snapshot, plugin-pin syntax with the file: vs link: pitfall, default fixture isolation, probe parallelism, absolute briefPath in v1). → [`2026-05-01-documentation-manifest-authoring-guide-laboratory-c-momaaa3t.md`](../records/2026-05-01-documentation-manifest-authoring-guide-laboratory-c-momaaa3t.md)
31	  - ☐ *(task → todo.md)* Port the first real-world trial — pick whichever P3 experiment is next (likely X016 orientation suppression), author its manifest, run it through the apparatus end-to-end. Validates the apparatus on real research work and produces the first real findings via the new pipeline.
32	  - ○ **Probe registry + extraction-dispatch contract — probe engines self-declare an extract() handler; nsg lab trial-extract dispatches by engineId via the registry rather than a hardcoded mapping. MVP-required: gates the trial-extract tool. Includes the registry pattern, the contract shape (extract(trialId, targetDir, guild) → file list), and built-in registrations for lab.probe-stacks-dump, lab.probe-git-range, and lab.probe-trial-context.** — Implemented as a structural type-guard pattern over the Fabricator's existing engine registry — no separate probe registry. ProbeEngineDesign extends EngineDesign with extract(args:{trialId, targetDir}) → {files:[{path, bytes}]}; isProbeEngineDesign type guard checks for the function. → [`2026-05-01-probe-registry-extraction-dispatch-contract-probe-c-momkil4p.md`](../records/2026-05-01-probe-registry-extraction-dispatch-contract-probe-c-momkil4p.md)
33	  - ○ **Implement lab.archive engine — read probe yields from upstream context, write one row to lab-trial-archives with id+trialId+archivedAt+probes[{id, engineId, summary}]. Atomic per-row via SQLite. Replace the current archive-presence safety check on teardown engines from 'context.upstream.archive defined' to 'archive row exists for this trialId'. Conclude this click on implementation; design conclusion lives at sibling c-momaa5o9 and full spec at packages/laboratory/README.md.** — Implemented at packages/laboratory/src/archive/engine.ts. Reads probe summaries from context.upstream (PROBE_ID(probe.id) keys), pairs each probe declaration with its yield via buildProbeEntries (preserves declaration order regardless of crawl order, throws on missing upstream — orchestration bug surface, wraps non-object yields in {value:...} so summary is always an object), writes one atomic row to lab-trial-archives with {id (generated), trialId (FK), archivedAt (ISO), probes:[{id, engineId, summary}]}. → [`2026-05-01-implement-labarchive-engine-read-probe-yields-from-c-momkqtn5.md`](../records/2026-05-01-implement-labarchive-engine-read-probe-yields-from-c-momkqtn5.md)
34	  - ● Failed-trial orphan cleanup — when guild-setup fails after codex-setup succeeds, the rig fails without running teardowns, leaving codexes registered in the lab-host's Scriptorium and bare repos on disk. Two design directions: (1) setup engines grab a release-on-rig-terminal handle that fires on any rig terminal-state, OR (2) teardowns run unconditionally on rig failure (currently they only run when the upstream chain succeeded). Surfaced during live verifies. Cosmetic for now (orphans don't run code) but failed-trial dev loops accumulate cruft.
35	  - ● Daemon vs CLI Scriptorium state drift — "nsg codex remove" from the CLI runs in a separate process and updates guild.json, but the running daemon in-memory Scriptorium state is not notified. The daemon next guild.json write reverts the orphan removals. Vibers currently has three cosmetic orphan codexes (verify-fixtures-{1267530e,2244ce82,40dd2663}) that will only clear on daemon restart. Cosmetic (orphan bare repos do not run code) but means CLI-side cleanup does not stick. Possible fixes: (1) CLI-side codex-remove signals the daemon via Clockworks event to reload Scriptorium, OR (2) Scriptorium adopts a watch-and-reconcile pattern on guild.json changes, OR (3) document the daemon-restart requirement and fail-loud when CLI-side codex changes are made with a daemon running.
36	- ◇ *(parked)* v2: introduce 'experiment' as a grouping concept above trial writs — when comparative views (cross-trial dashboards, integrating verdict, multi-variant analysis) earn their keep. Likely a new writ type that owns child trial writs, or a separate 'studies' book. Hold until comparative-analysis features have a motivating need.
37

Architecture: experiment as first-class research unit; trial-runs as writ-shaped execution units; fixtures (setup/teardown with a dep DAG) decoupled from probes (data extraction); declarative scenarios + end-conditions; per-experiment analysis instruments. Lab runs in a 'real' guild that owns all experiment data; temp test-guilds are pure observation surfaces. Generalizes the P3 infrastructure spec (c-molveg89) into a reusable apparatus. Reuses the 'Laboratory' name (data-mirroring stub retired 2026-04-30 is internal-only).
